mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.42% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.6% is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which together predict reduced corporate earnings growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 513356M and HOUST at 1139K show recent stability
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized weakness across consumer and construction
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.18 reflects recent manufacturing activity
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment and rise in unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate hiring confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 151095K and UNRATE at 3.6% show labor market resilience
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with synchronized employment and unemployment stress
failure_condition: Joint labor market deterioration fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP growth over a 2-quarter window signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18912.3B reflects ongoing economic expansion
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI rises above 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 254.96 and FEDFUNDS at 2.42% indicate accommodative real rates
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with negative real rates
failure_condition: Negative real rates fail to predict positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in industrial production and decline in payroll employment signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that reduces productive capacity and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% AND PAYEMS declines > 30K month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.18 and PAYEMS at 151095K show recent stability
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor market contraction
failure_condition: Joint supply-labor deterioration fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.
